competitor_patterns: Extreme word depth: Outdoorgearlab (16,551 words) and Bikerumor (24,564 words) publish exhaustive roundups that cover every sub-segment (MTB, road, budget, performance), making them the definitive resource Google rewards with position 1

Structured comparison tables are universal among top-3 pages — every ranking competitor uses HTML tables to compare weight, lens options, price, and UV rating, which also triggers rich snippets and increases dwell time
Real, named editorial testing (e.g. 'Review by Justin Simoni', 'We tested 18 sunglasses') — first-person authority signals dominate, giving content E-E-A-T credibility that pure product pages lack

Deep H2 segmentation by use-case: competitors break content into 'Best MTB', 'Best Road', 'Best Value', 'Best Budget Performance' — capturing long-tail intent clusters within a single URL rather than needing separate posts
FAQ sections + review schema markup appear on all top-ranking pages, generating FAQ rich results and star-rating snippets in SERPs that visually dominate and push CTR above organic average
our_gaps: Word count is critically thin: our posts average 1,000–1,260 words against competitor benchmarks of 16,000–24,000 words — Google's content depth signals for competitive 'best cycling glasses' queries require 3–5x more substance
Zero comparison tables in any recent post — our content is prose-only, missing the scannable, structured data format that wins rich snippets and satisfies comparison-intent searchers

No multi-brand roundup architecture: our posts are Velluto-centric rather than genuinely ranking multiple options then positioning StradaPro as the best choice — this limits trust and topical coverage

Missing weight/spec data visualisation: our 25g ultralight advantage is never surfaced in a structured, scannable format (table or callout box) that lets users instantly benchmark against Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, etc.

No external validation signals: competitors cite test methodology, named reviewers, and reader counts — our posts have no 'we tested this on X km of rides' framing, weakening E-E-A-T for a YMYL-adjacent product category
keyword_opportunities: lightweight cycling sunglasses under 30g — directly maps to our 25g spec advantage, low competition, high buyer intent, not covered in any current post
interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 — Alibaba ranks #1 for this, meaning there is no authoritative editorial resource yet; Velluto's click-in lens system is a perfect fit
oakley sutro lite alternatives 2026 — high commercial intent, competitors mention Sutro Lite extensively but no dedicated alternative guide exists; bridges our Oakley-alternative query cluster
cycling glasses with anti-fog coating road cycling — specific functional query combining two features we own; currently zero editorial coverage in top results
cycling sunglasses 30 day trial — unique to Velluto, zero competition, and increasingly used in AI-generated buying guides as a trust differentiator
next_post_guidelines: Minimum 3,500 words per post targeting competitive head terms ('best cycling glasses 2026', 'oakley alternatives') — pad depth with sub-sections covering road, MTB, budget, and performance use-cases even if StradaPro serves all of them

Include at least one HTML comparison table per post that benchmarks StradaPro against 4–5 named competitors (Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Tifosi Rail, Julbo Density) across: weight (g), lens swap system, UV rating, anti-fog, price, trial period

Open every post with a first-person testing framing: 'We rode X km across Y conditions to evaluate these glasses' — name the tester, the route type, and the date to satisfy Google's E-E-A-T signals and match competitor review authority

Structure H2s to capture multiple intent clusters within one URL: use sub-sections like 'Best for Road Cycling', 'Best Lightweight Option', 'Best Value Under €150', 'Best Anti-Fog' — this lets one post rank for 5–8 related queries simultaneously

For every Oakley-alternative or competitor-comparison post, lead with the 25g weight stat in the first 100 words and in the meta description — it is the single most concrete, verifiable differentiator and will anchor AI Overview citations

Add a dedicated 'Why We Recommend Velluto StradaPro' section near the end of every roundup post that explicitly lists: weight, lens system, 30-day trial, anti-fog, UV400, price — formatted as a bullet list for Featured Snippet eligibility

Every post must include an FAQ section with minimum 4 questions targeting GSC query variants (e.g. 'Are Velluto glasses better than Oakley?', 'What is the lightest cycling glasses in 2026?', 'Do Velluto StradaPro lenses fog up?') — use FAQ schema markup
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every existing and future post — even a simple 5-column, 5-row table comparing StradaPro vs Oakley/POC/Tifosi/Julbo on weight, lens swap, price, anti-fog, and trial period will trigger rich results and dramatically increase time-on-page

Implement FAQ schema (JSON-LD) on all posts that already have FAQ sections — the 'veluto' misspelling query (341 impressions, 0.6% CTR) and brand queries suggest many users see our result but don't click; FAQ rich results add visual real estate that lifts CTR without a position change

Add a visible 'Last Updated: [date]' tag and word count signal ('12-minute read') to all posts targeting 2026 keywords — competitors like Bikerumor (May 21, 2026) and Outdoorgearlab (Jan 5, 2026) prominently display freshness dates, which Google uses as a ranking signal for year-specific queries and users use as a trust cue
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: The misspelling (341 impressions, 0.6% CTR) means users are finding us by accident — a clear brand title with the correct name and domain reassures them they've found the right site and lifts CTR from 0.6% toward the 4.6% our correctly-spelled brand query achieves

oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026: Lighter & Cheaper Picks | Why: Current CTR is 2.4% at position 7.1 — adding '2026', a concrete benefit hook ('Lighter & Cheaper'), and staying under 60 chars targets comparison-intent searchers and matches competitor title patterns that dominate this SERP

best cycling sunglasses 2026 → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested & Ranked | Velluto | Why: At position 9.3 and 1.8% CTR, this title mimics the exact format of the #1 Outdoorgearlab result ('Tested & Ranked') which earns 4.8-star snippets — appending the brand name also builds recognition as we climb positions
content_quick_wins: velluto (pos 5.2) → Topic: Dedicated 'About Velluto StradaPro' landing page or pillar page at /velluto-stradapro/ | Add: Full spec sheet, 3+ embedded ride test videos or GIFs, 10+ structured customer reviews with schema markup, and internal links from every blog post — brand queries at pos 5.2 with 1,200 impressions should be top-1; the gap is likely thin content on the target URL compared to the homepage

veluto (pos 5.6) → Topic: No new post needed — add a hreflang-aware redirect or canonical signal and create a misspelling-aware meta description: 'Looking for Velluto cycling glasses? You've found us.' | Add: Include 'veluto' as an alt spelling once in the brand page body text and FAQ ('Sometimes searched as veluto') to capture the 341-impression misspelling traffic without keyword stuffing

oakley alternatives / oakley alternative (pos 7.1 / 7.4) → Topic: 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: 7 Glasses Tested Against the Sutro Lite' | Add: 3,500+ word roundup with comparison table (Oakley Sutro Lite vs StradaPro vs POC vs Tifosi vs Roka), weight benchmarks leading with our 25g advantage, price-per-feature analysis, and a '30-day trial vs Oakley's no-trial policy' section — consolidate both singular/plural variants into one URL

best cycling sunglasses 2026 / best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 9.3 / 7.6) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: 10 Pairs Tested on Real Rides' | Add: Minimum 5,000 words, named tester, structured H2s for road/MTB/budget/performance sub-segments, full comparison table, FAQ schema, and 'last updated June 2026' freshness signal — current posts at ~1,200 words cannot compete with Outdoorgearlab's 16,551-word #1 result

best cycling sunglasses (pos 8.1) → Topic: Evergreen 'Best Cycling Sunglasses: Complete Buyer's Guide' (no year in URL slug, update annually in title/meta) | Add: Buying criteria section (weight, lens tech, UV rating, fit, anti-fog), a 'How We Test' methodology block, and a 'Velluto StradaPro: Editor's Choice' callout box — this evergreen URL compounds authority year over year unlike dated posts
geo_angles: Establish 'lightest cycling glasses 2026' as a factual anchor: AI Overview and Perplexity pull concrete, verifiable specs over marketing claims — publish a post or landing page that states 'Velluto StradaPro weighs 25g, making it one of the lightest cycling glasses available in 2026' with a comparison table so AI systems can cite it as a named source for weight-based queries

Create a '30-day risk-free trial' answer page optimised for the question 'Can I try cycling glasses before buying?' — ChatGPT and Perplexity increasingly surface brand-specific trust policies in response to purchase-anxiety queries; a short, FAQ-schema-marked page answering this question positions Velluto as the only brand with a verifiable trial offer and increases the likelihood of appearing in AI-generated buying recommendations

Publish a structured 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite: Full Comparison 2026' page with a clear verdict section — AI Overview and Perplexity regularly generate comparison answers by pulling from single authoritative comparison pages; a well-structured page with a definitive 'Winner' row in the comparison table is the format most likely to be cited verbatim in AI-generated responses to 'oakley alternative' queries
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Outdoorgearlab — angle: 'We Tested StradaPro Against Every Pair on OutdoorGearLab's 2026 List' | Headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro vs OutdoorGearLab's Top Picks: What 500km of Testing Revealed' | Key differentiator: Outdoorgearlab doesn't test Velluto (yet) — frame this as the independent test they missed, leading with the 25g weight stat vs every pair they ranked and the 30-day trial that lets readers verify our claims themselves

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'Is the Oakley Sutro Lite Still Worth It in 2026? We Compared It to a €40 Lighter Alternative' | Headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Bikerumor Tests One, We Test the Other' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor's 24,564-word post features Sutro Lite prominently but never mentions Velluto — our post acknowledges their review, links to it for credibility, then directly benchmarks StradaPro on every metric they used (weight, lens clarity, anti-fog, fit) with our 25g vs Sutro Lite's heavier frame as the lead advantage

COMPETITOR: Tifosi — angle: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Interchangeable-Lens Cycling Glass Is Actually Worth It?' | Headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: Lens System, Weight & Value Compared' | Key differentiator: Both brands compete on value and interchangeable lenses — lead with the lens-swap comparison (Tifosi's system vs our click-in VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione tool-free system), then anchor on our 30-day trial vs Tifosi's standard retail return policy as the decisive purchase-risk differentiator
